original: Sydney McLaughlin-Levrone, American hurdler and sprinter (4x Olympic Gold medals and world record holder, born in New Brunswick, New Jersey
Viking 2 enters Martian orbit after an 11-month flight from Earth
Scientists in Pasadena, California, announce Viking I found strongest indications to date of possible life on Mars
Thor Heyerdahland the crew of the Kon-Tiki crash into a reef in the Tuamotu Islands, French Polynesia, after 101 days crossing the Pacific Ocean
Lynne Cox becomes the first woman to swim from the US to the Soviet Union, 4.3 km across the Bering Sea in water temperatures averaging 43-44°F (6-7°C)
19 kittens, world record for domestic cat litter, born to Burmese/Siamese cat Tarawood Antigone in Kingham, born in Oxfordshire, England (4 were stillborn)
Explorer 6 transmits the first TV photo of Earth from space
Cosmonaut Gherman Titov circles Earth for a full day in Vostok 2
Douglas D-558-II Skyrocket reaches 1,992 km/h (1,237.77 mph)
Mount Usu volcano in Hokkaido, Japan erupts for the first time in over 30 years
Apollo 15 returns to Earth
Takao Doi, Mamoru Mohri, and Chiaki Mukai are chosen to be Japan's first astronauts
Brandon Sanderson's Kickstarter for the "Cosmere RPG" game becomes the most successfully funded game in Kickstarter history, raising $41.7 million
EnglishmanRoger Bannisterbeats Australia's John Landy in the mile at the Empire Games in Vancouver, the first time two men run a sub-4-minute mile in the same race
American Alice Coachman becomes the first Black woman from any country to win an Olympic gold medal when she wins the high jump at the London Games
American swimmerKatie Ledeckysets a new world record with a time of 3:56.46 to win the gold medal in the women's 400 m freestyle at the Rio de Janeiro Olympics
British swimmerAdam Peatysets a world record of 57.13 to win the gold medal in the men's 100 m breaststroke at the Rio de Janeiro Olympics
Grete Waitzof Norway wins inaugural women's marathon at the first World Athletics Championships in Helsinki, Finland
Indian javelin thrower Neeraj Chopra makes history by winning his country's first-ever Olympic track and field gold with a throw of 87.58 m in Tokyo
As part of the winning American 4 × 400 m relay team in Tokyo, Allyson Felix takes her total to 11 Olympic medals and becomes the most decorated female Olympian in track and field history
Sarah Sjöström of Sweden breaks the world record with a time of 55.48 to win the gold medal in the women's 100 m butterfly at the Rio de Janeiro Olympics
Japan beats US, 6-3 in the final of the Los Angeles Olympic baseball demonstration event
The US collects its first Olympic gold medal in women's basketball history with a 85-55 win over South Korea in the final at the LA Games
Kevin Durant, with 29 points, leads the USA to his third and the team's fourth consecutive Olympic men's basketball gold medal with an 87-82 win over France in Tokyo
Don Larsen, AmericanMLBpitcher who hit the only perfect game in World Series history in Game 5, 1956 (World Series MVP 1956; NY Yankees), born in Michigan City, Indiana (d. 2020)
NY Yankees sluggerBabe Ruthties MLB record by hitting grand slams in consecutive games for the second time in a 13-1 win against the Philadelphia Athletics
The Orlando Magic signs NBA No. 1 draft pickShaquille O'Neal
First all-computer chess championship is conducted in New York and won by CHESS 3.0 (CDC 6400), a program written by Slate, Atkin, and Gorlen at Northwestern University
IBM dedicates the first program-controlled calculator, the Automatic Sequence Controlled Calculator (known best as the Harvard Mark I)
English set alight eight fireships with pitch, brimstone, gunpowder, and tar and cast them downwind towards the closely anchored vessels of the Spanish Armada, scattering the Armada

Commander of the Continental Army,George Washington, creates two Honorary Badges of Distinction and a Badge of Military Merit (now the Purple Heart). First time military awards are presented to common soldiers.
Construction begins on the dome of Florence Cathedral, designed by Renaissance architect Filippo Brunelleschi
Alice Ramsey (22) and three friends become the first women to complete a transcontinental automobile trip
Dan Buettner, Bret Anderson, Martin Engel, and Anne Knabe begin a cycling journey of 15,266 miles from Prudhoe Bay, Alaska, to Argentina
The US Viking rocket reaches a height of 210 km, setting a record
Mendocino Fire becomes the largest recorded fire in California's history at 290,600 acres, overtaking the 2017 Thomas Fire
First American offensive in Pacific in WW2, Guadalcanal, Solomon Island
Anna Haining Swan, Canadian giantess at 7ft 11in (exhibited with P. T. Barnum's Museum), born in Mill Brook, Nova Scotia (d. 1888)[1]
Tokyo Telecommunications Engineering, the precursor to Sony, begins selling its first transistor radios in Japan
Sidney Crosby, Canadian hockey player, born in Nova Scotia, Canada
Kyler Murray, American football quarterback (Heisman Trophy 2018, U of Oklahoma; 2019 NFL Draft #1 pick Arizona Cardinals), born in Bedford, Texas
Bruce Dickinson, British heavy metal rocker (Iron Maiden - "Run to Hills"), born in Worksop, England
Single "Tik Tok" is released byKesha(Billboard Song of the Year 2010)
Fantasy Records releases "Green River", the third studio album by the American rock bandCreedence Clearwater Revival, featuring the title track and "Bad Moon Rising", and "Lodi"
Jerry Rice,Emmitt Smith, John Randle, Russ Grimm, Rickey Jackson, Floyd Little, and Dick LeBeau are inducted into the Pro Football Hall of Fame
Alan Page, American football defensive tackle (NFL MVP 1971; 6×First-team All-Pro; 9×Pro Bowl; Minnesota Vikings) and Associate Justice Minnesota Supreme Court (1993–2015), born in Canton, Ohio
Yelena Davydova, Russian gymnast (Olympic gold USSR All-round, Team 1980), coach (Canadian Women's Artistic Team 2012) and international judge (Rio Olympics 2016), born in Voronezh, Russia
Kent Rominger, American astronaut (STS 73, 80, 85), born in Del Norte, California
James Allison, American immunologist (Noble Prize for Medicine 2018), born in Alice, Texas
Franciscus Henri [Antheunis], Dutch-Australian singer songwriter of childrens' music ("Ducks Like Rain"), and television personality, also known as 'Mister Whiskers', born in The Hague, Netherlands
B.J. Thomas, American singer ("Raindrops Keep Fallin' On My Head"; "Hooked On A Feeling"; "Growing Pains Theme"), born in Hugo, Oklahoma (d. 2021)
Rahsaan Roland Kirk, American jazz multi-instrumentalist (Bright Moments; The Case of the 3 Sided Dream in Audio Color), born in Columbus, Ohio (d. 1977)
Carl "Alfalfa" Switzer, American actor (Our Gang; Beginner's Luck; General Spanky), born in Paris, Illinois (d. 1959)
M. S. Swaminathan, Indian agricultural scientist, leader of the green revolution in India, born in Kumbakonam, Madras Presidency (d. 2023)
Rudolf Ising, American cartoonist (Looney Tunes; Merrie Melodies), born in Kansas City, Missouri (d. 1992)
Louis Alan Hazeltine, American inventor (neutrodyne circuit, making radio possible), born in Morristown, New Jersey (d. 1964)
(Mary) "Billie" Burke, American radio, stage, silent and sound film actress (Zieigfield Follies; The Wizard of Oz; Merrily We Live), born in Washington, D.C. (d. 1970)
Louis de Freycinet, French explorer and navigator (published the first map to show a full outline of the coastline of Australia in 1811), born in Montélimar, Drôme (d. 1842)
Larisa Karlova, Ukrainian handball player (Olympic gold USSR 1976, 80; World C'ship gold 1982, 86), born in Kyiv, Ukraine
Raúl Malo, American singer-songwriter, guitarist and record producer (The Mavericks - "Dance the Night Away", "What a Crying Shame"), born in Miami, Florida
